title: Silky Hangover-Cure Brazilian Fries Wrap
description: A hangover-cure Brazilian dish featuring fries, nigiri-shaping, and wrapped in a pandan leaf. This dish is low-glycemic-friendly and packed with nutrients.

ingredients.name: Sweet potato fries
ingredients.notes: Low-glycemic sweet potatoes are used to reduce blood sugar spikes.

ingredients.name: Cohosh root
ingredients.notes: Used for its anti-inflammatory properties to reduce hangover symptoms.

ingredients.name: Coconut oil
ingredients.notes: Used for frying and adding flavor.

ingredients.name: Pandan leaves
ingredients.notes: Used as a wrap to add flavor and nutrients.

ingredients.name: Tapioca starch
ingredients.notes: Used as a coating to make the fries crispy.

ingredients.name: Nutritional yeast
ingredients.notes: Used to add a cheesy flavor and boost B vitamins.

ingredients.name: Guarana powder
ingredients.notes: Used for its caffeine content to help alleviate hangover symptoms.

ingredients.name: Parsley
ingredients.notes: Used as a garnish to add flavor and freshness.

ingredients.name: Coconut milk
ingredients.notes: Used as a sauce to add creaminess and flavor.

ingredients.name: Brazilian cheese
ingredients.notes: Used to add flavor and texture.

ingredients.name: Chili flakes
ingredients.notes: Used to add heat and flavor.

instructions: Cut the sweet potatoes into long, thin strips and soak them in cold water for at least 30 minutes.
Dry the sweet potato strips with paper towels and coat with tapioca starch.
Heat the coconut oil in a deep frying pan to 160°C and fry the sweet potato strips in batches until crispy.
Drain the fried sweet potato strips on paper towels and season with salt and black pepper.
Prepare the cohosh root by grinding it into a fine powder.
Mix the cohosh root powder with coconut milk to create a sauce.
Heat the pandan leaves in a pan over low heat and wrap each sweet potato strip in a pandan leaf.
Place a spoonful of the cohosh root sauce on top of the sweet potato strip and wrap the pandan leaf around it to create a nigiri-shaped wrap.
Garnish with parsley and chili flakes.
